questions: Identify the web analytics organization that began donating its crawl data to the Internet Archive in 1996. Once identified, consult the collection details to specify the frequency with which this data flows into the archive, the temporal condition applied before it appears in the Wayback Machine, and the current public accessibility status of the specific 'DR Crawl' data subset.

Identify the web analytics organization that began donating its crawl data to the Internet Archive starting in 1996. Regarding the 'Plus' service associated with the brand name of this organization, what specific purchase requirement grants 'Early Access', and what verbal command can existing device owners use to request this access?

rounds.question: Identify the web analytics organization that began donating its crawl data to the Internet Archive in 1996. Once identified, consult the collection details to specify the frequency with which this data flows into the archive, the temporal condition applied before it appears in the Wayback Machine, and the current public accessibility status of the specific 'DR Crawl' data subset.
checklist_width: Data Flow Frequency: Flowing in every day (daily)
Availability Condition: Added after an embargo period
DR Crawl Accessibility: Currently not publicly accessible
checklist_depth: Logic Proof: Matches the organization donating crawl data to Internet Archive starting in 1996
rounds.rationale: The question uses Deep Logic by masking 'Alexa Internet' behind its historical relationship and start date (1996) with the Internet Archive. It uses Wide Logic by requiring the retrieval of three distinct attributes (frequency, embargo status, and specific subset accessibility) derived from the collection's metadata.
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Organization: Alexa Crawls Starting in 1996, Alexa Internet has been donating their crawl data to the Internet Archive. Flowing in every day, these data are added to the Wayba
rounds.answer_a:
Alexa Internet began donating its crawl data to the Internet Archive in 1996. The donated data, specifically the 'DR Crawl' subset, flows into the archive continuously as the crawling process occurs [1]. However, there is a 9-month temporal condition applied; the data does not become publicly accessible in the Wayback Machine until nine months after the crawl date [1]. According to the Internet Archive's collection details, this specific 'Alexa Internet - Web Archive (DR Crawl)' dataset is currently marked as publicly accessible [2].

## References
[1] Internet Archive, "Alexa Internet – Web Archive (DR Crawl)", Collections Overview, accessed July 18, 2024, https://archive.org/details/alexa-drcrawl
[2] Internet Archive, "Alexa Internet - Web Archive (DR Crawl)", Collection Information, accessed July 18, 2024, https://archive.org/details/alexa-drcrawl?tab=collectioninformation
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A correctly identified the target entity (Alexa Internet) and addressed the specific constraints regarding data flow and temporal conditions. However, Agent A hallucinated the accessibility status, claiming the 'DR Crawl' data is publicly accessible, whereas the provided ground truth states it is not. Agent B failed to generate a response entirely. While Agent A is the clear winner over a non-response, the factual error prevents a 'Much Better' rating per the rubric.

lines: -> WINNER is A. Targeting Loser Failure: BOTH.

checklist_width: Purchase Requirement: Buy an eligible device to get Early Access
Verbal Command: 'Just ask Alexa for access to Alexa+'
checklist_depth: Target Entity: Alexa Internet (or Amazon Alexa/Alexa brand)
Logic Proof: Identified via the history of donating crawl data to the Internet Archive starting in 1996.

It then applies Wide Aggregation to retrieve specific promotional details (Target 0) regarding 'Alexa+' Early Access conditions and verbal triggers, which are not explicitly named in the reasoning setup.

rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent B represents a catastrophic failure in User Experience: it outputted a raw internal tool call (XML/code) rather than a final response to the user. Agent A, while far from perfect (it provided only a list of references without a summary body, and the citations appear to be hallucinations tailored to the prompt), at least provided a readable output that contained the specific keywords and answers requested in the Ground Truth (Alexa Plus, Device Purchase, Command). Agent A is the clear winner by default because Agent B failed to generate a response.
